label: Self-Interaction Sweep
description: A packaged Campaign Studio study that varies only engine.g_int so a fresh user can inspect how the self-interaction control changes stability, density, runtime, and recommendation evidence.
purpose: Turn the new QS-DMSS scientific-scope lane into a runnable tangible-utility demo: one small deterministic self-interaction campaign that can be run, compared, verified, bundled, and exported.
expected_runtime: Fast laptop/CI campaign; five 8 x 8 x 8 NumPy runs are intended to finish in under a minute on typical reviewer hardware.

metrics.label: Energy drift
metrics.description: Primary stability signal used by the default recommendation profile.

metrics.label: Norm drift
metrics.description: Conservation-style sanity check for the deterministic reference run.

metrics.label: Max density
metrics.description: Compact peak-density response that may change as engine.g_int varies.

metrics.label: Elapsed seconds
metrics.description: Small-run runtime signal used to keep the demo reviewer-friendly.

limitations: Small deterministic reference campaign, not a calibrated physics result.
Uses a compact grid and short step count for fast inspection rather than production resolution.
Varying engine.g_int here demonstrates workflow sensitivity and evidence handling; it does not validate a dark-matter model.
non_claims: Does not prove one self-interaction value is scientifically correct.
Does not replace peer-reviewed numerical validation, physical calibration, or production cosmology codes.
Does not claim large-scale structure, observational, or astrophysical inference.

interpretation.summary: QS-DMSS varies engine.g_int while holding the grid, seed, initial packet, timestep, and step count fixed. The cockpit should be read as a reproducible parameter-study walkthrough: compare drift, peak density, verification status, and recommendation rationale across the campaign.
what_changed: Only engine.g_int changes across the packaged campaign variants.
Energy drift and norm drift show whether this small numerical setup stayed stable enough for inspection.
Max density shows whether the final density concentration changed across the interaction sweep.
Elapsed seconds helps keep the study honest about reviewer-facing runtime.
metric_meanings: Energy drift is the primary stability metric in this template; lower absolute drift is preferred.
Norm drift is a conservation-style sanity check; large drift would deserve numerical review.
Max density is an output response, not a claim of physical correctness.
The recommendation is an objective-driven ranking under the template scoring contract, not a scientific verdict.
what_this_does_not_claim: It does not prove an optimal physical self-interaction strength.
It does not validate QS-DMSS against peer-reviewed cosmological benchmarks.
It does not make production-scale claims beyond this small reproducible study.
interpretation.review_prompt: A useful review comment can focus on whether this g_int sweep makes the actual simulation behavior understandable, whether the metrics are sufficient, or which diagnostic would make the study more trustworthy.

dimensions.label: Interaction
dimensions.description: Sweep the nonlinear interaction term to compare stability and density response.

objective.name: Self-interaction stability and density response
objective.summary: Prefer verified runs with low drift while preserving enough density response to make the self-interaction sweep inspectable.

objective.name: Self-interaction stability and density response
objective.summary: Prefer verified runs with low drift while preserving enough density response to make the self-interaction sweep inspectable.

objective.summary: Prefer verified runs with low drift while preserving enough density response to make the self-interaction sweep inspectable.
